plain_definition: A technology-neutral Swiss statute, in force since 1 January 2020, licensing portfolio managers, trustees, securities firms and other financial institutions. FinIA contains no crypto-specific licence category: portfolio managers and trustees who manage or hold crypto assets on a discretionary, commercial basis for clients need a FinIA licence and supervision by a FINMA-authorised supervisory organisation, and securities firms dealing in FINMA-classified asset tokens need a FinIA securities-firm licence, on the same terms as for any other financial instrument. FinIA is also the statute a pending Federal Council proposal (consultation closed 6 February 2026, not yet enacted) would amend to insert two new licence categories, 'payment instrument institutions' and 'crypto-institutions', replacing the Banking Act's art. 1b fintech licence.
formal_definition: Federal Act on Financial Institutions (Financial Institutions Act, FinIA/FINIG), SR 954.1, in force since 1 January 2020.
